DECLARATION OF CATHERINE E. KOSS IN SUPPORT OF OPPOSITION TO MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES AND REQUEST FOR DISCOVERY SANCTIONS FILED BY DEFENDANT HAWTHORNE/STONE REAL ESTATE INVESTMENTS, INC., A BUSINESS ENTITY CASSIL, ROB ,AN INDIVIDUAL
MINI-MINUTES FOR MAY-09-2011 9:00 AM FOR DEPT 301

DISCOVERY 301, A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS HAWTHORNE/STONE REAL ESTATE INVESTMENTS, INC., AND ROB CASSIL'S MOTION TO COMPEL PLAINTIFFS' FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION, SETS ONE., PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 2030.300, 2031.310 IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF MUST RESPOND FULLY TO INTERROGATORIES CONCERNING MEDICINE AND PROVIDER VISITS OR STATE THAT THEY ARE NOT SEEKING DAMAGES FOR SUCH MEDICINE, VISION, OR THE CONDITIONS ADDRESSED BY THEM. RANDALL MUST ANSWER INTERROGATORY 28, IDENTIFYING HIS CAREGIVERS. PLAINTIFF MUST PROVIDE VERIFICATIONS FOR ALL RESPONSES AND AMENDED RESPONSES NOT ALREADY VERIFIED. PLAINTIFF MUST RESPOND TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION IDENTIFIED IN DEFENDANT'S REPLY AS STILL AT ISSUE BY IDENTIFYING AND PRODUCING ALL RESPONSIVE DOCUMENTS UNLESS THEY HAVE ONLY BECAUSE THEY RECEIVED THEM FROM DEFENDANTS AS DISCOVERY RESPONSES OR STATING THAT DESPITE DILIGENT SEARCH AND EFFORTS THEY HAVE NONE. PLAINTIFF MUST EITHER MAKE ALL DOCUMENTS BEING PRODUCED AVAILABLE TO DEFENDANTS FOR COPYING OR MUST COPY AND PRODUCE THEM WITH AN INVOICE FOR THE COPYING CHARGES. PLAINTIFF MUST DO EACH THING ORDERED WITHIN 30 DAYS AND MUST PAY SANCTIONS TO DEFENDANTS IN THE AMOUNT OF $1,040.00. SOCIAL SECURITY NEED NOT BE PRODUCED. PREVAILING PARTY IS TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, NOT REPORTED (301/PJB)

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Pltf'S Further Responses To Special Interrogatories And Req.'S For Production, Sets One
Off Calendar May-09-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES - ATTY. BENNY MARTIN, TEL. NO. 558-1760 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. CATHERINE E. KOSS OF WILSON ELSER, TEL. NO. 625-9257 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS HAWTHORNE/STONE REAL ESTATE INVESTMENTS, INC., AND ROB CASSIL'S Motion To Compel Plaintiffs' Further Responses To Special Interrogatories And Request For Production, Sets One., Pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure Section 2030.300, 2031.310 is Granted. Plaintiff must respond fully to interrogatories concerning medicine and provider visits or state that they are not seeking damages for such medicine, vision, or the conditions addressed by them. Randall must answer Interrogatory 28, identifying his caregivers. Plaintiff must provide verifications for all responses and amended responses not already verified. Plaintiff must respond to Request for Production identified in Defendant's reply as still at issue by identifying and producing all responsive documents unless they have only because they received them from Defendants as discovery responses or stating that despite diligent search and efforts they have none. Plaintiff must either make all documents being produced available to Defendants for copying or must copy and produce them with an invoice for the copying charges. Plaintiff must do each thing ordered within 30 days and must pay sanctions to defendants in the amount of $1,040.00. Social Security need not be produced. Prevailing party is to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Not reported = (301/PJB)

MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO ADMISSION/INTERROGATORIES
Off Calendar May-09-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. BENNY MARTIN, TEL. NO. 558-1760 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. CATHERINE E. KOSS OF WILSON ELSER, TEL. NO. 625-9257 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND RULED AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFFS GWEN WOMACK, ROBERT RANDALL AND LYELL PERRY'S MOTION TO COMPEL DEFENDANT'S FURTHER RESPONSES TO DISCOVERY AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTION IS GRANTED. DISCOVERY MOTION IS GRANTED TO THE EXTENT RECORDS DEAL WITH CONDITIONS THAT ARE AT ISSUE. Defendant must produce the unredacted YARDY documents within 10 days and pay Plaintiff sanctions in the amount of $340.00. Prevailing party is to submit a proposed form of order. Judge: Peter J.
